paperTitle: A Kinect-based conducting system that leverages gestural control for the real-time visualization of musical structures, primarily for educational purposes, and evaluates its user experience with professional conductors, musicians, and general users.
centralContribution: The paper presents a Kinect-based conducting system that leverages gestural control for the real-time visualization of musical structures, primarily for educational purposes, and evaluates its user experience with professional conductors, musicians, and general users. This system provides an immersive tool for practicing conducting and understanding complex musical pieces without a live orchestra, offering audiovisual feedback via particle systems and gesture trajectory.

units.title: Kinect Conducting System
units.summary: Kinect-based system for real-time visualization of musical structures for edu...

content.caption: Main components of the Kinect-based conducting system.
content.description: The system leverages a Kinect sensor and Unreal Engine to visualize musical structures in real-time for educational use, offering an immersive tool for conducting practice.

units.whyThisUnit: This unit represents the core innovation and artifact of the paper, detailing the overall system architecture that is the central contribution.

units.title: Challenges in Conducting
units.summary: Existing systems lack accurate gesture recognition and engaging visualization...

content.description: Existing systems struggle with accurate gesture recognition, satisfactory 3D visualization of musical structures, and adequate support for aspiring conductors without a physical ensemble, motivating this research.

units.conceptualOrigin: Section II — Related Work, Problem Statement
units.whyThisUnit: This unit defines the problem space, highlighting the limitations of previous systems that the paper's contribution aims to overcome.
These items explain how musical characteristics are translated into real-time visual feedback using particle systems, a core method of the system.

content.description: Visual representation of pitch (particle color), intensity (particle size), and timbre (3D object shapes as emitters).

frames.label: Pitch to Color
frames.description: Castel’s 1734 color mapping translates musical pitch to particle color for visual feedback.

frames.label: Intensity to Size
frames.description: Particle size scales dynamically with musical intensity, allowing visual perception of loudness.

frames.label: Timbre to Emitters
frames.description: 3D object shapes (e.g., trombones) act as particle emitters, representing instrumental timbre.

content.description: This component synchronizes audio and visual outputs, mapping extracted musical data from MIDI files to their real-time 3D visual representations.

units.title: Gesture Recognition Algorithm
units.summary: Algorithm processes Kinect hand joint data to identify conducting gestures us...

content.description: An algorithm, based on Caramiaux et al., processes Kinect hand joint data by dividing gesture paths into segments and using dot product for similarity to identify conducting gestures.

units.conceptualOrigin: Section IV — System Implementation, Gesture Recognition
units.whyThisUnit: This unit details the technical foundation for how the system interprets user movements, a critical method for interactive control.
These units present the methodology and quantitative results of the system's evaluation, demonstrating its practical performance and user experience.

content.description: A study with 18 participants (professional conductors, musicians, ordinary users) performed 11 tasks to evaluate controls, gesture recognition, and dynamic/tempo modification.
Average time and median attempts for selected tasks (Task 1, Task 10) across user groups, revealing performance differences.
content.title: Task Performance: Selected Metrics
